science.title: Breakthrough Technique Reveals Atomic Secrets of Record-Breaking Superconductors
science.summary: Scientists have achieved a first in studying lanthanum superhydrides, a class of materials that could help unlock superconductivity at much higher temperatures. science.title: Scientists create supercharged vitamin K that helps the brain heal itself
science.summary: Scientists in Japan have created powerful new vitamin K-based compounds that may help the brain regenerate lost neurons — a breakthrough that could one day change how diseases like Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s are treated. By combining vitamin K with components related to vitamin A, the researchers developed compounds t

science.title: Scientists Challenge a 70-Year-Old Theory of Language With a Surprising Discovery
science.summary: A new study challenges the widely accepted idea that word meanings are organized around emotion. After analyzing billions of words, scientists found that language may be shaped by something more basic: the need for safety. Researchers at the University of Vermont have found a new way to understand language, challenging

science.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
science.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds.

science.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
science.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. tech.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
tech.summary: The botnet was reportedly tied to a Russia-based residential proxy network.

tech.title: Startup offers free home cleaning—if it can record it all for robot training
tech.summary: The latest twist in paying humans to wear head cameras for robot training data.

tech.title: After Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
tech.summary: Chipmaker Groq is looking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it pivots from hardware to focus more on AI inference, the process of refining the way AI models respond to prompted requests, per Axios.

tech.title: Microsoft under fire for threatening security researcher with criminal investigation

medicine.title: New drug could finally stop deadly fatty liver disease

medicine.title: Teclistamab extends remission in relapsed myeloma, with 70% progression-free at 18 months

medicine.title: RNA therapy for genetic heart failure moves closer to patients after lab gains

medicine.title: One in five patients achieve functional hepatitis B cure after 24 weeks of bepirovirsen
medicine.summary: In an editorial published in the New England Journal of Medicine, University of Michigan Health hepatologist Anna S. Lok, M.D., hails newly announced results of the B-Well clinical trials as "a major step toward a functional cure for hepatitis B virus infection."

astronomy.title: Red dwarf stars are cosmic killers that eat their own planets
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have discovered the first evidence that tiny red dwarf stars can devour their own planets.

astronomy.title: Close Encounter: Jupiter and Venus
astronomy.summary: The two brightest planets in our sky will be less than 2 degrees apart on June 9th at sunset. The post Close Encounter: Jupiter and Venus appeared first on Sky & Telescope.
